narration: Here's something stores will never tell you — when you're stressed or sad, you spend 30% more. And they are counting on every single dollar.
visual: Person silhouette slumped on couch holding phone, shopping cart filling up on-screen. '30%' counter animates up from 0. Orange '$3,200/YEAR' tag fades in at bottom.

narration: Buying something triggers the same dopamine hit as eating sugar. Your brain calls it relief — but the bill always shows up the next morning.
visual: SVG brain with orange glowing reward center pulsing. Six dopamine dots radiate outward. 'SAME RUSH AS EATING SUGAR' label appears, then '"RELIEF"' in large orange text.

narration: Retailers track your emotional state through browsing patterns — late Friday, just got bad news, post-breakup. That's when the targeted ads hit hardest.
visual: Four trigger tags spring in from left: STRESSED, SAD, BORED, LATE FRIDAY. Phone on right shows targeted ad popup flashing orange border. 'YOUR MOOD IS THEIR AD SIGNAL' banner.

narration: 75% of emotional purchases are regretted within 24 hours. You bought for the feeling — and the feeling was already gone by morning.
visual: Large '75%' fades in with subtitle 'OF EMOTIONAL BUYS'. Orange regret bar fills to 75%. Package box with orange X through it appears. 'THE DOPAMINE WAS THE POINT' banner.

narration: The average American blows $3,200 a year on emotional buys. Invested at 8% instead, that's $363,000 gone over 30 years — from feelings.
visual: Dollar counter rises from $0 to $3,200. Bar chart builds: 10Y=$46K, 20Y=$146K, 30Y=$363K in orange bars. '$363K STOLEN BY FEELINGS' banner appears.

narration: Next time the urge hits, close the app and wait 48 hours. 70% of those urges just vanish — and that one pause puts $2,240 back in your pocket. Follow for more.
visual: Clock SVG with hand spinning. Two rule cards appear: 'WAIT 48 HOURS / CLOSE THE APP' and '70% OF URGES DISAPPEAR / $2,240 SAVED'. Orange CTA follow button.
